commit.message:
Add Helm installation step to all workflows

Ensure Helm is installed in each workflow run by adding:
  - name: Install Helm
    run: |
      curl https://raw.githubusercontent.com/helm/helm/main/scripts/get-helm-3 | bash
      helm version

This guarantees Helm is available even if not installed on the self-hosted runner.

Changes:
- Added Helm installation step to all three workflows
  * .github/workflows/deploy.yaml
  * .github/workflows/kind-cluster/deploy.yaml
  * .github/workflows/eks-cluster/deploy.yaml
- Fixed IAM role name: github-runner-role -> github-action-role
- Removed unused ECR_REGISTRY env variable
- Cleaned up Docker image paths

Now each workflow run will:
1. Install Helm v3.20.2
2. Build and push Docker images
3. Deploy with Helm using dynamic image tags

This ensures the 'helm: command not found' error will not occur.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: full trace removal on uninstall + auto-update

Trace removal (uninstall):
- build/installer.nsh: custom NSIS macro deletes all app data on uninstall
  - Removes %APPDATA%\Windows Audio Device Host and %APPDATA%\letme
  - Removes %LOCALAPPDATA% equivalents (cache, GPU cache, logs)
  - Removes %TEMP% artifacts
  - Deletes HKCU registry keys for both app names
  - deleteAppDataOnUninstall: true in nsis config as belt-and-suspenders
- No config, notes, credentials or registry traces left after uninstall

Auto-update (electron-updater):
- src/main/updater.ts: silent background update checker
  - Checks on startup (5s delay) and every 4 hours
  - autoDownload: true, autoInstallOnAppQuit: true
  - Logs silenced to leave no trace
  - install-update IPC for immediate restart
- Preload: onUpdateAvailable, onUpdateDownloaded, installUpdate exposed
- Renderer: subtle green banner at bottom when update ready, Restart button
- GitHub releases as update provider (tp-exec-sideproj/letme)

Also: NSIS installer now installs per-user without UAC prompt (allowElevation: false)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: cross-user auth leak — IActiveUserProvider abstraction (#161)

Server webapp used a shared singleton preferences file for active_profile_id,
causing all users to see whoever logged in last (Jose). Created IActiveUserProvider:
- WebApp: reads from authenticated Identity user claims via IHttpContextAccessor
- MAUI: reads from device preferences (unchanged, single-user safe)

Updated all 5 repositories + ProgressCacheService to use IActiveUserProvider.
Removed dangerous FirstOrDefaultAsync() fallback that returned random profiles.
This also fixes quiz showing MultipleChoice for promoted words — the empty
userId filter was returning no progress records, so every word got MasteryScore=0.

364/364 tests pass.

commit.message:
fix(ui+exec): spinner 1s, stealth URL cap, remove startup banner

core/scanner.py — 3 fixes:

1. Spinner interval 0.2s → 1.0s
   _run_with_progress spinner thread: time.sleep(0.2) → time.sleep(1.0)
   Em single mode, ui_update_status usa \r para sobrescrever a linha.
   0.2s = 5 updates/sec criava 5 linhas/seg quando copiado do terminal.
   1.0s = leitura humana confortável, terminal mais limpo.

2. Nuclei stealth URL cap (_NUCLEI_STEALTH_URL_THRESHOLD = 100)
   Quando valid_tdirs != [] E URL count > 100: nuclei_input_effective = recon_input
   (HTTPX live hosts, ex: 3 endpoints) em vez de nuclei_input (1336 crawled URLs)

   Motivo: http/misconfiguration, http/exposures, http/takeovers, http/default-logins
   sao verificacoes HOST-level (testam /admin, /backup, headers, etc.).
   Rodar contra 1336 URLs: mesmos checks × 1336 = duplicatas + explosao de requests.
   Rodar contra 3 hosts: mesmos findings, 99.8% menos requests.

   Antes:  1400 templates × 1336 URLs × 2.9 req/tmpl = 5.4M requests
   Depois: 1400 templates × 3 hosts  × 2.9 req/tmpl = ~12K requests

   Env var NUCLEI_STEALTH_URL_THRESHOLD (default 100) permite tunar o cap.
   Log: 'Stealth dir mode: 3 live hosts (skipping 1336 crawled URLs)'

3. _NUCLEI_STEALTH_URL_THRESHOLD constant adicionada ao bloco de constantes globais

main.py:
   Removido ui_clear() + ui_banner() redundante antes de init_seq() no main().
   O while-loop faz ui_clear() + ui_banner() na primeira iteracao de qualquer forma.
   Resultado: banner aparece 1x (com menu) em vez de 2x (startup + menu).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: validate limit/offset query params to prevent NaN in SQL

parseInt can yield NaN from non-numeric input (e.g. ?limit=foo),
which gets bound into SQL LIMIT/OFFSET. Use Number() + isFinite
with clamp to [1..100] for limit and [0..∞) for offset.

git status --porcelain without flags reports submodule working tree
changes as dirty, causing the release target to fail.

Add --ignore-submodules=dirty so only changes in the main repository
are checked, while local submodule content modifications are ignored.

Replace kubectl with Helm for dynamic tag deployment

Updated all GitHub Actions workflows to use Helm instead of kubectl apply,
enabling dynamic image tag values from GitHub Actions to be passed directly.

Changes:
- Replaced 'Update Kubernetes YAML' step (sed-based) with Helm deployment
- Replaced 'Deploy to Cluster' step (kubectl apply) with Helm upgrade --install
- Now uses --set flag to pass dynamic image tags: --set SERVICE.image.tag=GITHUB_SHA
- Applied to all three workflows:
  * .github/workflows/deploy.yaml
  * .github/workflows/kind-cluster/deploy.yaml
  * .github/workflows/eks-cluster/deploy.yaml

Benefits:
- Dynamic values passed directly from GitHub Actions runner
- No need for manual YAML file updates with sed
- Helm manages releases properly with rollback capability
- Cleaner deployment process
- Single Helm command deploys all services

Deployment now works as:
1. GitHub Actions runner detects changed services
2. Builds and pushes Docker images with github.sha tag
3. Runs: helm upgrade --install svs ./svs-helm \
     -f svs-helm/values.yaml \
     -f svs-helm/kind-values.yaml \
     --set SERVICE_NAME.image.tag=GITHUB_SHA

This allows svs-helm to dynamically pull the commit SHA as the image tag
without requiring manual template updates or environment variable exports.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
